item.description: Biography Mr. Zhen Zhang is a postdoctoral research fellow in the Department of Computer Science at the National University of Singapore under the supervision of Prof. Lee Wee Sun. Previously, he recieved a PhD in Computer Science from Northwestern Polytechnical University (Xi’an, ChinaNWPU) under the supervision of Prof. Yanning Zhang. During Nov. 2012 to Dec. 2014, he was a visiting student at Australian Centre for Visual Technologies, University of Adelaide, under the supervision of Prof.

item.title: Dynamic Programming Bipartite Belief Propagation For Hyper Graph Matching
Graph Matching through Max-Weight Bipartite Belief Propagation
item.description: Abstract Graph matching is a key problem in computer vision and pattern recognition. One way to encode the essential interdependence between potential feature matches is to cast the problem as inference in a graphical model (pairwise model for graph matching and higher-order for hyper graph matching problems). Though recently alternatives such as spectral methods, multt-linear relaxations, or approaches based on the convex-concave procedure have achieved the state-of-the-art. In this project, we revisit the use of graphical models for feature matching, and propose a belief propagation scheme which exhibits the following advantages: (1) we explicitly enforce one-to-one matching constraints; (2) we offer a tighter relaxation of the original cost function than previous graphical-model-based approaches; (3) our sub-problems decompose into max-weight bipartite matching, which can be solved efficiently, leading to orders-of-magnitude reductions in execution time; (4) for hyper-graph matching problem, the dynamic programming technique can be used to further accelerate the algorithm.

item.title: MAP Inference Over Constrained Markov Random Field
Solving Constrained Combinatorial Optimisation Problems Via MAP Inference Without High-order Penalties
Real-time Correlation Filter Tracking by Efficient Dense Belief Propagation with Structure Preserving
Pairwise Matching through Max-Weight Bipartite Belief Propagation
Joint Probabilistic Matching Using m-Best Solutions
Learning graph structure for multi-label image classification via clique generation
Variable Kernel Density Estimation Based Robust Regression And Its Applications
Constraint reduction using marginal polytope diagrams for MAP LP relaxations
